# UC-22.32.5 · Cross-border transfer legal basis validation

## Description

Supports Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (LGPD) compliance evidence for cross-border transfer legal basis validation in Splunk dashboards and scheduled reports.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for LGPD compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes grc and sourcetypes lgpd:tia. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Maintain `amer_authorization_boundary.csv` with ATO, CUI, and CJIS scope flags. (2) Integrate POA&M exports via scheduled output and lookup refresh for ISSM workflows. (3) Separate LGPD and federal datasets with index- and role-level controls. (4) Quarterly control mapping workshop with ISSM, ISO, and privacy counsel. Indexes required: `index=grc`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=lgpd:tia`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (3110), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Cloud audit trails, Windows Security Event Logs, vulnerability scan outputs, access control logs, encryption status reports, continuous monitoring feeds, incident response logs, privacy management platforms, law enforcement system logs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain `amer_authorization_boundary.csv` with ATO, CUI, and CJIS scope flags. (2) Integrate POA&M exports via scheduled output and lookup refresh for ISSM workflows. (3) Separate LGPD and federal datasets with index- and role-level controls. (4) Quarterly control mapping workshop with ISSM, ISO, and privacy counsel.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc sourcetype="lgpd:tia" earliest=-365d
| stats latest(status) as st by transfer_id, dest_country
| where st!="Approved"
```

Understanding this SPL

**Cross-border transfer legal basis validation (Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (LGPD))** — Supports Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (LGPD) compliance evidence for cross-border transfer legal basis validation in Splunk dashboards and scheduled reports.

Documented **Data sources**: Cloud audit trails, Windows Security Event Logs, vulnerability scan outputs, access control logs, encryption status reports, continuous monitoring feeds, incident response logs, privacy management platforms, law enforcement system logs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (3110), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: grc; **sourcetype**: lgpd:tia. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=grc, sourcetype="lgpd:tia", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by transfer_id, dest_country** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where st!="Approved"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Cross-border transfer legal basis validation (Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (LGPD))** — Supports Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (LGPD) compliance evidence for cross-border transfer legal basis validation in Splunk dashboards and scheduled reports.

Documented **Data sources**: Cloud audit trails, Windows Security Event Logs, vulnerability scan outputs, access control logs, encryption status reports, continuous monitoring feeds, incident response logs, privacy management platforms, law enforcement system logs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (3110), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the GRC system and the audit register, and with regional reporting cutoffs in your runbook. Document any expected regulatory reporting window or approved exception before escalating.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked bar (control family), Table (users and events), Single value (open findings).

## SPL

```spl
index=grc sourcetype="lgpd:tia" earliest=-365d
| stats latest(status) as st by transfer_id, dest_country
| where st!="Approved"
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Stacked bar (control family), Table (users and events), Single value (open findings). Key fields: `dest_country`, `st`, `transfer_id`.
